volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Hecuba (Section 1000 - Section 1127b)

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: ἔστʼ , ὦ φιληθεὶς ὡς σὺ νῦν ἐμοὶ φιλῇ —
sections.translation: O you who are as dear to me as you now are , there are —

sections.label: Section 1001
sections.original: τί χρῆμʼ ὃ κἀμὲ καὶ τέκνʼ εἰδέναι χρεών ;
sections.translation: What is it that I and my children must learn ?

sections.label: Section 1002
sections.original: χρυσοῦ παλαιαὶ Πριαμιδῶν κατώρυχες .
sections.translation: Ancient vaults filled full of gold by Priam ’ s ancestors .

sections.label: Section 1003
sections.original: ταῦτʼ ἔσθʼ ἃ βούλῃ παιδὶ σημῆναι σέθεν ;
sections.translation: Is it this you would tell your son ?

sections.label: Section 1004
sections.original: μάλιστα , διὰ σοῦ γʼ · εἶ γὰρ εὐσεβὴς ἀνήρ .
sections.translation: Certainly , through you ; for you are a righteous man .

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: τί δῆτα τέκνων τῶνδε δεῖ παρουσίας ;
sections.translation: What need then of these children ’ s presence ?

sections.label: Section 1006
sections.original: ἄμεινον , ἢν σὺ κατθάνῃς , τούσδʼ εἰδέναι .
sections.translation: It is better they should know it , in case of your death .

sections.label: Section 1007
sections.original: καλῶς ἔλεξας · τῇδε καὶ σοφώτερον .
sections.translation: Well said ; it is also the wiser way .

sections.label: Section 1008
sections.original: οἶσθʼ οὖν Ἀθάνας Ἰλίας ἵνα στέγαι ;
sections.translation: Then do you know where the shrine of Trojan Athena is ?

sections.label: Section 1009
sections.original: ἐνταῦθʼ ὁ χρυσός ἐστι ; σημεῖον δὲ τί ;
sections.translation: Is the gold there ? what is there to mark it ?

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: μέλαινα πέτρα γῆς ὑπερτέλλουσʼ ἄνω .
sections.translation: A black rock rising above the ground .

sections.label: Section 1011
sections.original: ἔτʼ οὖν τι βούλῃ τῶν ἐκεῖ φράζειν ἐμοί ;
sections.translation: Is there anything else you want to tell me about the place ?

sections.label: Section 1012
sections.original: σῶσαί σε χρήμαθʼ οἷς συνεξῆλθον θέλω .
sections.translation: I wish to keep safe the treasure I brought from Troy .

sections.label: Section 1013
sections.original: ποῦ δῆτα ; πέπλων ἐντὸς ἢ κρύψασʼ ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: Where can it be ? inside your dress , or have you hidden it ?

sections.label: Section 1014
sections.original: σκύλων ἐν ὄχλῳ ταῖσδε σῴζεται στέγαις .
sections.translation: It is safe among a heap of spoils within these tents .

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: ποῦ δʼ ; αἵδʼ Ἀχαιῶν ναύλοχοι περιπτυχαί .
sections.translation: Where ? This is the station built by the Achaeans to surround their fleet .

sections.label: Section 1016
sections.original: ἰδίᾳ γυναικῶν αἰχμαλωτίδων στέγαι .
sections.translation: The captive women have huts of their own .

sections.label: Section 1017
sections.original: τἄνδον δὲ πιστὰ κἀρσένων ἐρημία ;
sections.translation: It is safe to enter ? are there no men about ?

sections.label: Section 1018
sections.original: οὐδεὶς Ἀχαιῶν ἔνδον , ἀλλʼ ἡμεῖς μόναι .
sections.translation: There are no Achaeans within ; we women are alone . Enter then the tent , for the Argives

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: λῦσαι ποθοῦσιν οἴκαδʼ ἐκ Τροίας πόδα ·
sections.translation: are eager to set sail from Troy for home ; and , when you have accomplished all that you must do , you shall return with your children to the place where you have lodged my son . Hecuba leads Polymestor and his children into the tent .

sections.label: Section 1024
sections.original: οὔπω δέδωκας , ἀλλʼ ἴσως δώσεις δίκην ·
sections.translation: Not yet have you paid the penalty , but perhaps you will .

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: ἀλίμενόν τις ὡς εἰς ἄντλον πεσὼν
sections.translation: Like one who slips and falls into the surge with no haven near , so shall you lose your own life for the life you have taken . For where liability to justice coincides with heaven ’ s law ;

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: ὀλέθριον ὀλέθριον κακόν .
sections.translation: there is ruin full of death and doom . Your hopes of this journey shall cheat you , for it has led you , unhappy wretch ! to the halls of death ; and to no warrior ’ s hand shall you resign your life .

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: ὤμοι , τυφλοῦμαι φέγγος ὀμμάτων τάλας .
sections.translation: O horror ! I am blinded of the light of my eyes , ah me !

sections.label: Section 1036
sections.original: — ἠκούσατʼ ἀνδρὸς Θρῃκὸς οἰμωγήν , φίλαι ;
sections.translation: Did you hear , friends , that Thracian ’ s cry of woe ?

sections.label: Section 1037
sections.original: ὤμοι μάλʼ αὖθις , τέκνα , δυστήνου σφαγῆς .
sections.translation: O horror ! horror ! my children ! 0 the cruel blow .

sections.label: Section 1038
sections.original: — φίλαι , πέπρακται καίνʼ ἔσω δόμων κακά .
sections.translation: Friends , there is strange mischief afoot in yo .

sections.label: Section 1039
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὔτι μὴ φύγητε λαιψηρῷ ποδί ·
sections.translation: No , you shall never escape for all your hurried flight ;

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: βάλλων γὰρ οἴκων τῶνδʼ ἀναρρήξω μυχούς .
sections.translation: for with a blow I will burst open the inmost recesses of this building .

sections.label: Section 1041
sections.original: — ἰδού , βαρείας χειρὸς ὁρμᾶται βέλος .
sections.translation: Hark ! how he launches a bolt with weighty hand ! Shall we force an entry ? The crisis calls on us to aid Hecuba and the Trojan women .

sections.label: Section 1044
sections.original: ἄρασσε , φείδου μηδέν , ἐκβάλλων πύλας ·
sections.translation: Strike on , spare not , burst the doors !

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: οὐ γάρ ποτʼ ὄμμα λαμπρὸν ἐνθήσεις κόραις ,
sections.translation: you shall never replace bright vision in your eyes or see your children , whom I have slain , alive again .

sections.label: Section 1047
sections.original: ἦ γὰρ καθεῖλες Θρῇκα , καὶ κρατεῖς , ξένον ,
sections.translation: What ! have you foiled the Thracian stranger and is he in your power , mistress ? Is all your threat now brought to pass ?

sections.label: Section 1049
sections.original: ὄψῃ νιν αὐτίκʼ ὄντα δωμάτων πάρος
sections.translation: A moment , and you shall see him before the tent ,

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: τυφλὸν τυφλῷ στείχοντα παραφόρῳ ποδί ,
sections.translation: blind , advancing with blind random step ; and the bodies of his two children whom I with my brave women of Troy killed ; he has paid me the penalty ; here he comes from the tent , as you see . I will withdraw out of his path and stand aside

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: θυμῷ ῥέοντι Θρῃκὶ δυσμαχωτάτῳ .
sections.translation: from the hot fury of the Thracian , my deadly foe .

sections.label: Section 1056
sections.original: ὤμοι ἐγώ , πᾷ βῶ ,
sections.translation: Woe is me ! where can I go , where halt , or turn ? shall I crawl like a wild four-footed beast on their track , as my reward ? Which path shall I take first ,

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.original: ἢ ταύταν ἢ τάνδʼ ἐξαλλάξω , τὰς
sections.translation: this or that , eager as I am to clutch those Trojan murderesses that have destroyed me ? You wretched , cursed daughters

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: ὦ κατάρατοι ,
sections.translation: of Phrygia ! to what corner have you fled cowering before me ? O sun-god , would you could heal , could heal my bleeding eyes , ridding me of my blindness !

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: ἆ ἆ ,
sections.translation: Ha ! hush ! I catch the stealthy footsteps of the women here . Where can I dart on them and gorge on their flesh and bones , making for myself a wild beasts ’ meal , inflicting mutilation

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: λύμας ἀντίποινʼ ἐμᾶς ; ὦ τάλας .
sections.translation: in requital of their outrage on me ? Ah , woe is me ! where am I rushing , leaving my children unguarded for maenads of hell to mangle , to be murdered and ruthlessly cast forth upon the hills , a feast of blood for dogs ?

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: πᾷ στῶ , πᾷ κάμψω , πᾷ βῶ ,
sections.translation: Where shall I stay or turn my steps , like a ship that lies anchored at sea , gathering close my linen robe and rushing to that chamber of death , to guard my children ?

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον , ὥς σοι δύσφορʼ εἴργασται κακά ·
sections.translation: Woe to you ! what grievous outrage has been done to you ! a fearful penalty for your foul deed has the deity imposed , whoever he is whose hand is heavy upon you .

sections.label: Section 1089
sections.original: αἰαῖ , ἰὼ Θρῄκης λογχοφόρον ἔνο-
sections.translation: Woe is me ! Ho ! my Thracian spearmen , armed ,

sections.label: Section 1090
sections.original: πλον εὔιππον Ἄρει κάτοχον γένος .
sections.translation: a race of knights whom Ares inspires ! Ho ! Achaeans ! sons of Atreus ! To you I loudly call ; come here , by the gods ! Does any one hearken , or will no one help me ? Why do you delay ? Women , captive women have destroyed me .

sections.label: Section 1095
sections.original: δεινὰ πεπόνθαμεν .
sections.translation: A fearful fate is mine ; ah me ! my hideous outrage ! Where can I turn or go ?

sections.label: Section 1100
sections.original: ἀμπτάμενος οὐράνιον
sections.translation: Shall I take wings and soar aloft to the mansions of the sky , where Orion and Sirius dart from their eyes

sections.label: Section 1105
sections.original: σιν ὄσσων αὐγάς , ἢ τὸν ἐς Ἀίδα
sections.translation: a flash as of fire , or shall I , in my misery , plunge to Hades ’ murky flood ?

sections.label: Section 1107
sections.original: συγγνώσθʼ , ὅταν τις κρείσσονʼ ἢ φέρειν κακὰ
sections.translation: It is pardonable , for a man suffering from evils too heavy to bear , to rid himself of a wretched existence .

sections.label: Section 1109
sections.original: κραυγῆς ἀκούσας ἦλθον · οὐ γὰρ ἥσυχος
sections.translation: Hearing a cry I have come here ; for Echo ,

sections.label: Section 1110
sections.original: πέτρας ὀρείας παῖς λέλακʼ ἀνὰ στρατὸν
sections.translation: child of the mountain-rock , has sent her voice loud-ringing through the army , causing a tumult . If we had not known that Troy ’ s towers were levelled by the might of Hellas , this uproar would have caused no slight panic .

sections.label: Section 1114
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ · ᾐσθόμην γάρ , Ἀγάμεμνον , σέθεν
sections.translation: Best of friends ! for by your voice I know you ,

sections.label: Section 1115
sections.original: φωνῆς ἀκούσας · εἰσορᾷς ἃ πάσχομεν ;
sections.translation: Agamemnon ; do you see my piteous state ?

sections.label: Section 1116
sections.original: ἔα ·
sections.translation: What ! hapless Polymestor , who has stricken you ? who has blinded your eyes , staining the pupils with blood ? who has slain these children ? whoever he was , fierce must have been his wrath against you and your children .

sections.label: Section 1120
sections.original: Ἑκάβη με σὺν γυναιξὶν αἰχμαλωτίσιν
sections.translation: Hecuba , helped by the captive women , has destroyed me — not destroyed , far worse than that .

sections.label: Section 1122
sections.original: τί φῄς ; σὺ τοὔργον εἴργασαι τόδʼ , ὡς λέγει ;
sections.translation: What do you say ? Was it you that did this deed , as he says ? You , Hecuba , that have ventured on this inconceivable daring ?

sections.label: Section 1124
sections.original: ὤμοι , τί λέξεις ; ἦ γὰρ ἐγγύς ἐστί που ;
sections.translation: Ha ! what is that ? is she somewhere near ?

sections.label: Section 1125
sections.original: σήμηνον , εἰπὲ ποῦ ʼσθʼ , ἵνʼ ἁρπάσας χεροῖν
sections.translation: Show me , tell me where , that I may grip her in my hands and rend her limb from limb , bespattering her with gore .

sections.label: Section 1127
sections.original: οὗτος , τί πάσχεις ;
sections.translation: You creature , what are you about ?

sections.label: Section 1127b
sections.original: πρὸς θεῶν σε λίσσομαι ,
sections.translation: By the gods I entreat you , let me vent on her the fury of my arm .
